metadata.assertion_changes: Tightened from iteration-1: Added IV numeric data check, historical valuation check, invalidation check (eval 1); added quantified leverage, historical averages, per-unit margin, dedicated commodity section (eval 2); raised options depth threshold from 5 to 20 lines, added numeric IV requirement, multi-strategy check, options-specific invalidation (eval 3)

notes: CRITICAL FINDING: The old skill (without_skill/baseline) OUTPERFORMS the improved skill on aggregate pass rate (97.4% vs 94.9%). The improved skill wins on Eval 2 (commodity-producer) but loses on Eval 3 (options-focused).

Eval 1 (MSFT): Both 16/16 (100%). Non-discriminating. Both configs found IV data and historical averages this time — the iteration-1 gaps were run variance, not skill-driven. Old skill produced a richer report (240 vs 186 lines) with more sources (26 vs 14).

Eval 2 (GOLD): Improved skill 13/13 vs old 12/13. The ONLY discriminating assertion: historical valuation averages. Improved skill found 42.9x 10yr P/E; old skill provided only peer averages. The improved skill's instruction to search for '[TICKER] historical PE ratio 5 year average' appears to have driven this. Both skills now quantify miner leverage (7-8% for 5% gold move) — the iteration-1 leverage gap was closed by the old skill this time.

Eval 3 (SOFI): REGRESSION. Improved skill 11/13 vs old 13/13. Two failures: (1) IV Rank/Percentile returned N/A despite skill explicitly instructing to search AlphaQuery/Barchart/MarketChameleon — agent searched AlphaQuery and found IV mean but not rank/percentile (paywalled). (2) Options section was only ~18 lines vs old skill's 22+. The old skill estimated IV Rank at '~60-75%' rather than reporting N/A, and produced a more expansive options section with a Put-Selling Decision Framework table.

PARADOX: The improved skill's instruction 'Returning N/A here when the user asked about options is a failure mode to avoid' may have had the OPPOSITE effect — the agent, more aware that N/A is unacceptable, was MORE honest about not finding the data rather than estimating. The old skill's agent, without this instruction, freely estimated from context. Consider rephrasing to: 'If exact IV Rank/Percentile is unavailable, estimate from context with clear caveats rather than returning N/A.'
Timing: Improved skill is faster on average (274s vs 340s), which is interesting — the more structured instructions may help agents focus rather than over-research.

The old skill continues to produce creative bonus sections not in the template: Put-Selling Decision Framework table (Eval 3), Stock Type Classification section (Eval 3), 'The Bottom Line for Your Question' section (Eval 2). The improved skill added a Put-Selling Checklist in Eval 3, partially addressing this, but the old skill's output is more expansive.
